children.heading: Alcohol-related neurodevelopmental disorders; ARND
children.label: 1 Alcohol-related neurodevelopmental disorders; ARND
children.text: The term “alcohol-related neurodevelopmental disorders” or “ARND” means, with a history of maternal alcohol consumption during pregnancy, central nervous system abnormalities, which may range from minor intellectual deficits and developmental delays to mental retardation. ARND children may have behavioral problems, learning disabilities, problems with executive functioning, and attention disorders. The neurological defects of ARND may be as severe as FAS, but facial anomalies and other physical characteristics are not present in ARND, thus making diagnosis difficult.

children.heading: Assessment
children.label: 2 Assessment
children.text: The term “assessment” means the systematic collection, analysis, and dissemination of information on health status, health needs, and health problems.

children.heading: Behavioral health aftercare
children.label: 3 Behavioral health aftercare
children.text: The term “behavioral health aftercare” includes those activities and resources used to support recovery following inpatient, residential, intensive substance abuse, or mental health outpatient or outpatient treatment. The purpose is to help prevent or deal with relapse by ensuring that by the time a client or patient is discharged from a level of care, such as outpatient treatment, an aftercare plan has been developed with the client. An aftercare plan may use such resources as a community-based therapeutic group, transitional living facilities, a 12-step sponsor, a local 12-step or other related support group, and other community-based providers.

children.heading: Dual diagnosis
children.label: 4 Dual diagnosis
children.text: The term “dual diagnosis” means coexisting substance abuse and mental illness conditions or diagnosis. Such clients are sometimes referred to as mentally ill chemical abusers (MICAs).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “fetal alcohol spectrum disorders” includes a range of effects that can occur in an individual whose mother drank alcohol during pregnancy, including physical, mental, behavioral, and/or learning disabilities with possible lifelong implications.

children.label: i
children.text: fetal alcohol syndrome (FAS);

children.label: ii
children.text: partial fetal alcohol syndrome (partial FAS);

children.label: iii
children.text: alcohol-related birth defects (ARBD); and

children.label: iv
children.text: alcohol-related neurodevelopmental disorders (ARND).

children.label: A
children.text: Central nervous system involvement, such as mental retardation, developmental delay, intellectual deficit, microencephaly, or neurological abnormalities.

children.text: Microophthalmia.

children.label: ii

children.label: iii

children.label: iv

children.label: v

children.label: vi

children.label: C
children.text: Prenatal or postnatal growth delay.

children.label: A

children.label: B

children.label: i
children.text: The maximum attainment of physical, mental, and developmental functioning.

children.label: ii
children.text: Averting deterioration in physical or mental functional status.

children.label: iii
children.text: The maintenance of physical or mental health functional status.

children.heading: Substance abuse
children.label: 8 Substance abuse
children.text: The term “substance abuse” includes inhalant abuse.
